programmes.show_description: 1961 Morris Minor 1000: Paul and Jack head to Staffordshire to pick up five vehicles from collector Rob's colossal barn, including a Mini and a Morris Minor.
Riley Falcon: Dave's in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox.

programmes.show_name: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: The New Taste for Blood: Lucy Worsley turns detective to explain our macabre fixation with killing. Hear how real-life crimes in the early 1800s gave society a thirst for murder.
Shopping from Home.
Laxey: Ben Robinson visits Laxey on the Isle of Man to discover the unusual attraction that drew Victorians to the tourist destination - the biggest waterwheel in the world.
Ravenglass: Ben Robinson discovers more about the quiet village of Ravenglass in the Lake District's National Park, which played a strategic role in the Roman empire.
Flamborough: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings.
Cley next the Sea: Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and finds due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, it's not as 'next the sea' as it once was.
Rollo Croftmaster Tractor: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep.
1924 Rover 9HP Tourer: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 that's been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that.
Porsche: The Story of how car designer Ferdinand Porsche became one of the most influential engineers in all Nazi Germany.
Into the Abyss: Most of Germany is occupied by invading Allied forces by April 1945, with a strong Soviet army now just a mile from Hitler's hiding place.
Media City UK 2: Fiona Bruce presents from MediaCityUK on the former Manchester Docks, where items brought in include an early animation machine and a magician's box of tricks.
Cromer Pier 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Cromer Pier in Norfolk, where items brought in include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death' and the Duke of Wellington's nightcap.
Jowett Javelin/Ford Escort: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave find a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years.
Bond Bug/Jag MK2: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2.

The adventurer travels to the vast white salt flats of Bolivia, through the world's greatest wetlands of Brazil's Pantanal, and on to the forested heart of Paraguay.

programmes.show_name: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motorcar
programmes.show_description: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car company's plant as work gets underway on a range of 4x4s, from the factory floor to the world's most exclusive car showroom.

programmes.show_name: This World: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to one of the most spectacular countries in the world, Colombia, to explore the country's precarious future.

programmes.show_name: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: Detection Most Ingenious: Lucy Worsley dissects society's dark obsession with slayings. Hear how true crime and the emerging art of detection piqued the interest of the Victorians.
Shopping from Home.
Tintagel: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwall's Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur.
Craster: Ben Robinson visits Northumberland's Craster to find out how a family who shares the name have had their lives and fortunes intertwined with the village for more than 800 years.
Orford: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unearths the Suffolk village of Orford's history of defending the British Isles.
Parkgate: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores Parkgate on the estuary of the River Dee in Cheshire, landlocked due to the estuary's shifting silt.
1954 Morgan Plus 4 Coupe: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan Plus 4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road.
1968 Ford Cortina: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills.
Opel: The story of the GM Opel Blitz and the American-owned war factories that would produce them, putting business before country.
Most Wanted: The return of the documentary, picking up in the immediate aftermath of the Second World War, when the hunt began to capture and convict the highest-ranking Nazis.
Eltham Palace 3: Fiona Bruce presents from Eltham Palace in Greenwich, south-east London, where items include a diamond bracelet and a tea set that went to Antarctica.
Compilation 2: Fiona Bruce presents highlights from the past 25 years of the show. Expert Marc Allum discusses some of his favourite finds.
Standard Ensign/MG TC: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and collection of tractors, but extracting them from a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated.
Moskovich/MGB GT: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskvitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics.

Simon Reeve travels from Chile's Atacama Desert and follows the Andes to Patagonia before ending up in most southerly inhabited region on earth, Tierra Del Fuego.
Austin A30 Seven/Hillman Imp: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.
Using cutting edge technology, Andrew and Brendan explore a 100-year-old mining claim, while a family connection leads Tanya to a different kind of treasure trove.
